title: Rally to Restore Rebuild Sanity
wikitext:
[]: Rally to Restore (Rebuild) Sanity             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sat Oct 30, 2010 4:14 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by Chrad » Sat Oct 30, 2010 10:50 am I agree about the 'third path' regarding the climax. Shinji hasn't reached some ultimate enlightenment, but Anno nonetheless wants us to experience the same emotional highs and lows along his path to accepting himself and others. I'd wager that the reason people think either that Shinji's actions are being totally endorsed or perceive this directorial choice as 'trolling' (other than groupthink and the "Anno is a troll!" meme) is that it's rare in mainstream media for a director to attempt to sweep up the audience in the emotions of a scene unless it's meant to be read some kind of ultimate triumph. As you say though, it's typical of Anno. Asuka's battle with the MP-Evas is not the peak of her character arc; it's not entirely reflective of what how she needed to change, but as you watch the scene it feels like it is, even as there are hints that something's wrong. It's the same with Shinji at the end of 2.0. We feel his personal sense of triumph, even as we know that it's temporary, and he is still far from realizing all that he should. Seele00TextOnly wrote: For us to be so well aware as we are that 'surely this isn't an actual triumph, this isn't what 'should' be happening' to me writes off immediately the notion that this is trolling, fanbaiting, etc.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Oct 30, 2010 11:47 am I still feel Kaworu wants to cause 3I on his or Seele's terms. Though somebody (Gendo'sPapa or SaltyJoe) suggested that Kaworu could make Shinji happy by Shinji learning to accept/love himself and others. Anno knew his message in NGE/EoE. I'd say he also knows his message in 1.11/2.22 and is gradually building up to it. (And assuming the last 2 films are 90-100 minutes, that's more than enough time to flesh out Asuka and Mari, battle the last 2 to 3 Angels, explain whatever's been unexplained, etc.)

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Sat Oct 30, 2010 12:51 pm I have to agree with the points being made so far in this thread. I've read a lot of detractors of Rebuild (or at least, 2.22) say that the ending of 2.22 gives off the completely wrong message because it seems supportive of Shinji's actions. I really don't think it's supportive. Everytime I watch that scene I get an enormous mixture of feelings; I'm happy for Shinji doing what he's doing but at the same time, he's basically triggered the end of the world. God knows how many people had been killed because of him. And considering we have at least one film to go, I don't think he has finished growing. From the preview at the end of 2.22, it looks that there are going to be dire consequences to his actions that will affect everyone. Although, I think it's also something borne out of the structuring of the film. One of my problems (and I don't know if I can call it that) with the series was that I think it took too much time in allowing its characters to grow, it kept dropping Shinji into nadirs that he seemed to have kicked himself out of. Well, okay, maybe it wasn't a flaw. Yes, it was a little repetitive but I think it did enhance the message of the original series and this kind of conflict was necessary for the other episodes and the film. That said, I just don't see that working in a film series. And I think that's one of the reasons why Anno has made those changes. However, I think he has changed the path for Shinji, not so much the destination but I think we will really have to wait and see.

[]:             Quote Post by Nonoriri » Sat Oct 30, 2010 1:00 pm I just hope that 3.0 not only acknowledges Shinji's massive shortcomings (or rather the idea that the one time his father gives him advice isn't the best time to start listening to him as it doesn't exactly bode well for humanity and just furthers the idea that Shinji and Rei hell everyone are mere pawns toward a larger aim) but actually beats him over the head with it. I have a feeling that it could go either way but I'd like the Gendo brand of selfishness to end as tragically as possible. Everything else is entering "new" territory and that can only be a good thing, the half-hearted, repackaged events of 1.0 and 2.0 really aren't the best groundwork for a supposed remake. Oh and development that comes after integral events is kinda antithetical to... uh character development . So I hope they have some actual character interaction going on there next time. It would also be nice if characters didn't exist in a vacuum that requires Shinji's presence or even name to be developed, I'd be nice if they existed on their own for a bit (even though they never really did in the series and that wasn't the point but hey )

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sat Oct 30, 2010 1:22 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Nonoriri » Sat Oct 30, 2010 1:57 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: I do totally agree with regards to what you want to see in the next films about Shinji and more character interactions, but I feel that what you said in this quote is mostly what we were given in the series, at least that is how it feels looking back. That most characters greatest developments occurred at their respective ends, and that previous developments had proven to be false starts or dead ends. Maybe I'm just viewing the original through the lens of 'uber tragedy' too much, I'm not sure. Then again, at least those previous developments had occurred and perhaps that gave the original more credit to crunch the majority of its defining developments into the final acts and moments. This horrendously long wait for the next installment is killing me... the way that it handles Shinji, Asuka, and Misato are going to be so paramount to the solidifying of my (and I feel most others) opinions about 2.22.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Oct 30, 2010 5:21 pm In some twisted way I have to admire Anno for having set up the latest movie so that Shinji manages to gather even more hate than he did for being a total muppet in EoE. I'd say that it might be the difference between sinning by omission and sinning by commission (arguments over "So everyone just die." aside).

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Oct 30, 2010 5:34 pm Mr. Tines wrote: I'd say that it might be the difference between sinning by omission and sinning by commission (arguments over "So everyone just die." aside).

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sat Oct 30, 2010 10:57 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Nonoriri » Sun Oct 31, 2010 2:12 am Seele00TextOnly wrote: Nonoriri: I agree with much of your sentiments actually to varying extents, except for most notably the feeling that Asuka (I assume you were talking about Asuka) was reduced to secondary character at any time. I have my own wacky interpretations of this that frankly I can't defend well or put into words, though I've alluded to it here and there... I'm quite convinced that Asuka is and will be the overall main character of Rebuild somehow. But of course that sounds stupid and crazy, so what else can I say on the matter? Not much, at least for now.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Sun Oct 31, 2010 2:22 am Literally just finished watching my 1.11 and 2.22 blurays back-to-back. It was a spirit quest fueled by the green herb. This is my third time seeing both, but first time as a single run. Thus I bring you... O-chan's special reflection on Rebuild as a complement to Neon Genesis Evangelion~ For me, the original series is a slow burn. The only way I watch it is over the course of a weekend, several episodes at a time. I finish it all off with End of Evangelion. It's a pretty damn satisfying experience. But I have to watch all the episodes in order, never in isolation. It has to be that slow burn. All those hours of viewing enjoyment make me feel like it's been quite a journey. Rebuild can't really compare on that level. Watching Rebuild (or these two films at least) is more of a constant barrage. Shit's constantly happening and every single image is gorgeous. I love the colours and the compositions and the sense of movement. I associate a very different mood and aura with Rebuild. But I think for me, Rebuild cannot exist on its own merit. If I wanted to be as objective as possible, I'd argue Rebuild is fairly mediocre on story/character. It's obviously delicious visually. But what use is me thinking objectively like that? After all, I watched the original TV series a bajillion times before I ever saw Rebuild. For that reason, Rebuild is complementary to Neon Genesis Evangelion. It is like a very special omake or something. When I think of Shinji, Rei, Asuka, Misato, et al., I'm always thinking of the original characters. I can't help but think of the Rebuild versions as extra thoughts on the originals. For me, I simply cannot divorce them from the originals, even if they're different. So the emotions I feel during Asuka's test disaster, or when Shinji's actually making connections with people, it is all tied up with what I feel for the originals. And really, I'm just happy to experience another slice of the Evaverse. I get to see Kaji and Misato in different circumstances, for example. I could imagine these scenes happening in the context of the original series. That's pleasing to me. I can appreciate Rebuild in the same way I can appreciate concept art, drafts, and things like that. I probably wouldn't give a shit about Rebuild if I'd never seen Neon Genesis Evangelion. But because I have, I'm able to enjoy Rebuild. That's how I think it all works for me. Oh yeah, and Rebuild is basically one long eyegasm. I swoon during the action scenes, but I also love all the details. Sometimes during 2.22 I just wanted to play it in slow motion. It is so visually satisfying. My eyes were never ever ever ever ever bored. Like, it is like a big giant beautiful art book. I get to see more images from the Evaverse. How could that not delight me? (I'd like to point out that this whole reflection is working on the assumption that Rebuild is not a "sequel". If it is, there's a good chance that my feelings on it could skyrocket or plummet.)

[]:             Quote Post by Chrad » Sun Oct 31, 2010 9:27 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: I still feel Kaworu wants to cause 3I on his or Seele's terms.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Sun Oct 31, 2010 1:18 pm Idea~! I don't know if it'd work here or in some other thread (or if this has already been done) but I almost want to compile a giant list of all the scenes, utterances, and happenings in the second film that are given additional meaning by being familiar with NGE. I can't help but think Anno-san wanted us to think along these lines.

I can't help but think Anno-san wanted us to think along these lines.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Mon Nov 01, 2010 9:35 am For the record, I will not be the one compiling this list because I am lazy and easily distracted. But yaaaay! I see Xard-kun and the person with a name as bizarre as mine are taking up the torch. This thread is making me happy. I am rubbing my glands on it.
